Definition (EN): A constraint definition is a kind of occurrence definition (see 7.9 ) that defines a logical predicate.
Context/Example (EN): The modeling of constraints, which specify conditions that can be evaluated as true or false, or asserted to be true or false (see 7.20 ).

Definition (EN): A constraint definition is a kind of occurrence definition (see 7.9 ) that defines a logical predicate.
Context/Example (EN): constraint def IsFull { in tank : FuelTank; tank.fuelLevel == tank.maxFuelLevel // Result expression }

Definition (EN): A constraint usage is an occurrence usage that is the usage of a constraint definition.
A logical predicate is a condition that can be evaluated as true or false.
Context/Example (EN): A constraint definition is a kind of occurrence definition (see 7.9 ) that defines a logical predicate.

Definition (EN): For a given set of input parameter values, a constraint usage is satisfied if its expression evaluates to true and is violated otherwise.
Context/Example (EN): E.g., if x is 3 and y is 5, then the expression x < y evaluates to true, and the constraint is satisfied.

Definition (EN): For a given set of input parameter values, a constraint usage is satisfied if its expression evaluates to true and is violated otherwise.
Context/Example (EN): For a given set of input parameter values, a constraint usage is satisfied if its expression evaluates to true and is violated otherwise.

Definition (EN): An assert constraint usage asserts that the result of a given constraint must be always true at all times.
An assert constraint usage asserts that the result of a given constraint must be always true at all times.
Context/Example (EN): Constraints associated with the laws of physics, for example, should be asserted to be true, because they cannot be violated in any valid model of the real world.

Definition (EN): A negated assert constraint usage can be used to assert that some condition must never happen if the model is logically consistent.
